summary.overall_assessment: Commercially strong, appetising, and mostly consistent food cut-out image system for marketplace/category use.
primary_strengths: Transparent background for flexible UI usage
Good recognisability for several hero categories
High retouch polish and modern marketplace feel
primary_risks: Some category overlap at small sizes
Shadow treatment slightly heavy in places
A few assets are less scalable as navigation symbols
Kebab asset appears weaker/lower consistency than the rest
summary.recommended_next_step: Turn the set into a stricter scalable image system with small-size variants, tighter silhouette rules, clearer differentiation for overlapping cuisine categories, and stricter item-count limits for grocery category assets.
summary.small_size_rule: For future grocery category images intended to read at very small sizes, keep to 2-3 items maximum.

images.label: Pizza
images.subject: Margherita pizza on parchment
images.composition: Large round hero object, angled top view, strong frame fill

images.label: American / Fried Chicken Burger
images.subject: Fried chicken burger on paper
images.composition: Centred stacked handheld with large bun silhouette
issues: Can overlap slightly with burgers category if labels are weak

images.label: Asian
images.subject: Mixed bowl with egg, vegetables, and meat
images.composition: Top/angled bowl composition with many components

images.label: Bakery
images.subject: Croissant with flakes
images.composition: Single pastry hero with strong diagonal silhouette

images.label: Breakfast
images.subject: Acai bowl with fruit and granola

images.label: Burgers
images.subject: Classic cheeseburger on paper
images.composition: Centred burger stack, heavy rounded bun silhouette

images.label: Burrito / Mexican
images.subject: Foil-wrapped burrito with visible filling
images.composition: Diagonal wrapped handheld with visible cross-section

images.label: Chinese
images.subject: Saucy rice bowl
images.composition: Bowl split between rice and sauced main
issues: Less distinctive than a more iconic Chinese cue
Can blend into broader Asian bowl categories

images.label: Coffee
images.subject: Hot coffee and iced coffee
images.composition: Paired drinks, one hot and one cold
what_works: Strong contrast between hot and iced
issues: Visual weight slightly lighter than food heroes

images.label: Desserts
images.subject: Cheesecake slice with berry topping
images.composition: Single plated dessert with strong triangular slice

images.label: Healthy
images.subject: Protein tray with grains and vegetables
images.composition: Compartment tray with balanced meal layout
issues: May not stand out in a dense grid

images.label: Ice Cream
images.subject: Scoops of chocolate and vanilla ice cream
images.composition: Simple bowl with rounded scoop forms

images.label: Kebab
images.subject: Wrapped kebab with chips and meat
images.composition: Diagonal wrapped item with paper wrap
issues: Feels lower resolution / different production batch

images.label: Korean
images.subject: Bibimbap-style bowl with egg and vegetables
issues: Can overlap with generic Asian bowl

images.label: Thai
images.subject: Green curry with herbs and chilli
images.composition: Wide bowl/plate with liquid curry surface
what_works: Fresh herb and chilli garnish helps
issues: Reads less clearly at small size

images.label: Vegan
images.subject: Plant-based bowl with avocado, quinoa, chickpeas and greens
images.composition: Top/angled bowl with multiple fresh components
issues: Overlaps visually with healthy and breakfast bowls
Needs stronger hierarchy for small-size use

highest_priority_actions: Clarify overlap between Asian, Chinese, Korean, Thai, Healthy, Vegan, and Breakfast
Standardise visual weight across all assets
For grocery category assets, cap compositions at 2-3 items to preserve very small-size readability

future_schema.tier_1: Hero assets for marketing and large modules
future_schema.tier_2: Category tile assets optimised for navigation
future_schema.tier_3: Micro assets for chips, filters, and small UI

small_size_spec.goal: Assets must remain recognisable in very small UI tiles
rules: Limit grocery category imagery to 2-3 items
Test recognition at tiny tile sizes before approval

grocery_category_image_rules.intended_use: Very small category tiles, shortcuts, and navigation surfaces
composition_rules: Arrange items as a tight grouped cluster
Ensure the group reads clearly at very small size
Avoid tiny secondary details that disappear when reduced
No item names or packaging text

uk: Use packaging formats familiar to the UK grocery market

recommended_items: whole milk style bottle or carton
example_for_milk.note: Keep the three items tightly grouped and simple enough to read instantly at tiny sizes.
